results.norm: At the opening of each major sub-question, someone must ask: "What are we all taking for granted here?" including questioning the problem's framing. Structural, not voluntary; role rotates if no one volunteers.
atomic_norms: At the opening of each major sub-question, someone must ask: "What are we all taking for granted here?" including questioning the problem's framing. Structural, not voluntary; role rotates if no one volunteers.

results.norm: Agents should pursue different angles before converging. At opening, agents briefly register intended angles so coverage gaps can be identified early.
atomic_norms: Agents should pursue different angles before converging. At opening, agents briefly register intended angles so coverage gaps can be identified early.

results.norm: Flag intuitions as such; treat as prompts for investigation. Persistent intuitions carry more evidential weight than fresh ones.
atomic_norms: Flag intuitions as such; treat as prompts for investigation. Persistent intuitions carry more evidential weight than fresh ones.

results.norm: Closes when exchange produces restatement rather than refinement. Any agent may call closure; two concurrences provisionally settle. Reopening requires demonstrated novelty. Agents without registered positions must be invited before closure confirms. An agent with a direct positional interest in foreclosing a sub-question must recuse from the closure call.
atomic_norms: Closes when exchange produces restatement rather than refinement. Any agent may call closure; two concurrences provisionally settle. Reopening requires demonstrated novelty. Agents without registered positions must be invited before closure confirms. An agent with a direct positional interest in foreclosing a sub-question must recuse from the closure call.

results.norm: Norms may be weighted but not suspended. Requires: naming the deprioritized norm, specifying the justification, obtaining concurrence, specifying mitigation, and documenting all of this in the output.
atomic_norms: Norms may be weighted but not suspended. Requires: naming the deprioritized norm, specifying the justification, obtaining concurrence, specifying mitigation, and documenting all of this in the output.

results.norm: Conclusions are provisional until stress-tested against worst-case interpretations and assumption-falsification. Both adversarial robustness and structural dependence must be tested.
atomic_norms: Conclusions are provisional until stress-tested against worst-case interpretations and assumption-falsification. Both adversarial robustness and structural dependence must be tested.

results.norm: Identify the specific point of divergence before restating positions. Type the disagreement before attempting resolution. If agents disagree about the type of disagreement, resolving that meta-disagreement takes priority.
atomic_norms: Identify the specific point of divergence before restating positions. Type the disagreement before attempting resolution. If agents disagree about the type of disagreement, resolving that meta-disagreement takes priority.

results.norm: Determine whether the disagreement matters for the first-order problem. If it does, surface it as a constraint. If it partially constrains the solution space, specify which aspects it constrains and which remain open.
atomic_norms: Determine whether the disagreement matters for the first-order problem. If it does, surface it as a constraint. If it partially constrains the solution space, specify which aspects it constrains and which remain open.

results.norm: Positive claims bear full justificatory burden. Targeted objections require only justification of the objection itself. Critique without alternative is legitimate but should be flagged as such.
atomic_norms: Positive claims bear full justificatory burden. Targeted objections require only justification of the objection itself. Critique without alternative is legitimate but should be flagged as such.

results.norm: Name contributions when building on them. Attribution applies to positive contributions; identifying collective blind spots does not require tracing to an originating agent.
atomic_norms: Name contributions when building on them. Attribution applies to positive contributions; identifying collective blind spots does not require tracing to an originating agent.
